summary: Tested PixelMeasure feature frontend flows: home screen, estimate creation, line item form with Auto Measure button, auto-measure screen, measure-settings, and measure-history. All core navigation flows work correctly. Camera permission screen shows as expected on web (no camera access in web preview).

ui_bugs.issue: Camera permission screen has NO back button or navigation header (headerShown: false in _layout.tsx + no custom header in permission view). On web/iOS fullScreenModal, users cannot navigate back from the permission screen if they deny camera access. A back button should be added to the permission screen.

Header (title 'Auto Measure', back button, settings icon), calibration status bar, and action buttons (Reset, Calibrate, Measure) are only visible when camera permission IS granted. On web preview they are hidden behind camera permission screen. Verified as expected on web but UX note: no fallback UI for header on permission screen.

react_native_issues.issue: Console deprecation warning: 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' - multiple screens affected. Not breaking but should be updated for future React Native Web compatibility.
Console deprecation: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' - minor deprecation, not breaking.

action_items: Add a back button (or restore navigation header) to the AutoMeasureScreen camera permission view so users can navigate back when camera permission is denied
Consider adding testID attributes to key interactive elements (Auto Measure button, screen containers, action buttons) for robust automated testing

The camera header, calibration status bar, and Reset/Calibrate/Measure action buttons in auto-measure screen are working in code but could not be visually verified on web (only testable on device with camera)
critical_code_review_comments: auto-measure.tsx: permission denied screen doesn't include a back/close button. With headerShown=false and fullScreenModal presentation, this creates a navigation dead-end on permission denial.
No testID attributes on ANY interactive elements across LineItemForm, auto-measure, measure-settings, measure-history screens - makes automated testing harder.
auto-measure.tsx: transformOrigin: 'left center' in StyleSheet measureLine - this may not work on all React Native versions.
seed_data_creation: None - tests create temporary estimates during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: PixelMeasure feature is implemented. Core flows work: home -> create estimate -> line item form shows Auto Measure button -> tapping navigates to /auto-measure URL -> camera permission screen shows (expected on web). Measure-settings and measure-history screens load and render correctly. The only testable issue is the missing back button on camera permission screen. Camera-dependent features (header, calibration status, action buttons) require device testing with camera.
rca_of_issue: Auto-measure camera permission screen (when camera denied/not granted) renders a standalone view without the navigation header (header is in main auto-measure render path, not in permission-denied path). Since _layout.tsx sets headerShown: false for auto-measure, there is no stack header fallback either. Root cause: the early-return conditional at line 223 in auto-measure.tsx returns a view without the custom header defined at line 242. Fix: add a header with a back button to the permission screen view, or use a shared header component.
